lede: JFK offers Hidden Disabilities Sunflower lanyards to travelers with non-visible disabilities such as autism, PTSD, hearing loss, or chronic conditions. Wearing a lanyard is a simple way to let airport staff know you may need a little extra help, time, or understanding during your journey. Lanyards are free and available at Information Centers in the Arrivals area of each terminal, pre-security. Hours: 6 AM to midnight ET Terminal 4 International Information Center: Open 24 hours We can also mail you a lanyard. To request a Sunflower Lanyard in advance of your trip, please e-mail us. Include the following information in your email request: Your first and last name Full address Date of travel Airport Requests must be sent a minimum of 14 days before travel. Lanyards can only be mailed to domestic addresses. We cannot guarantee that the lanyard will arrive in time for your trip. Participation is completely voluntary. The lanyard does not provide priority access or skip-the-line privileges, but staff will do their best to support you. Learn more about the Sunflower Program

We can also mail you a lanyard. To request a Sunflower Lanyard in advance of your trip, please e-mail us. Include the following information in your email request:
Requests must be sent a minimum of 14 days before travel. Lanyards can only be mailed to domestic addresses. We cannot guarantee that the lanyard will arrive in time for your trip.
Participation is completely voluntary. The lanyard does not provide priority access or skip-the-line privileges, but staff will do their best to support you.

links.text: Learn more about the Sunflower Program

paragraphs: For our travelers who may feel overwhelmed by the busy airport setting, including neurodivergent individuals and people with sensory sensitivies, sensory spaces offer a quiet, low-stimulation environment.
We're excited to offer these spaces in the near future!

links.text: Deaf or hard of hearing services

paragraphs: The Port Authority of New York and New Jersey 4 World Trade Center, 150 Greenwich Street New York, NY 10007 +1 (212) 435-7000
